### Religion

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Canary_Islands&action=edit&section=35 "Edit section: Religion")]

Main article: [Religion in Canary Islands](/wiki/Religion_in_Canary_Islands "Religion in Canary Islands")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/19/Candelaria_Basilica.jpg/250px-Candelaria_Basilica.jpg)](/wiki/File:Candelaria_Basilica.jpg)

[Basilica of the Virgin of Candelaria](/wiki/Basilica_of_Candelaria "Basilica of Candelaria") (Patroness of the Canary Islands) in [Candelaria](/wiki/Candelaria,_Tenerife "Candelaria, Tenerife"), Tenerife

The [Catholic Church](/wiki/Catholic_Church "Catholic Church") has been the majority religion in the archipelago for more than five centuries, ever since the [Conquest of the Canary Islands](/wiki/Conquest_of_the_Canary_Islands "Conquest of the Canary Islands"). There are also several other religious communities.

#### Roman Catholic Church

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Canary_Islands&action=edit&section=36 "Edit section: Roman Catholic Church")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a3/Semana_Santa_La_Laguna_80.jpg/250px-Semana_Santa_La_Laguna_80.jpg)](/wiki/File:Semana_Santa_La_Laguna_80.jpg)

Procession of [Holy Week](/wiki/Holy_Week "Holy Week") in [San Cristóbal de La Laguna](/wiki/San_Crist%C3%B3bal_de_La_Laguna "San Cristóbal de La Laguna")

The overwhelming majority of native Canarians are [Roman Catholic](/wiki/Roman_Catholic "Roman Catholic") (76.7%)[[130]](#cite_note-CIS2019Canarias-131) with various smaller foreign-born populations of other Christian beliefs such as [Protestants](/wiki/Protestants "Protestants").

The appearance of the [Virgin of Candelaria](/wiki/Virgin_of_Candelaria "Virgin of Candelaria") (Patron of Canary Islands) was credited with moving the Canary Islands toward Christianity. Two Catholic saints were born in the Canary Islands: [Peter of Saint Joseph de Betancur](/wiki/Peter_of_Saint_Joseph_de_Betancur "Peter of Saint Joseph de Betancur")[[131]](#cite_note-132) and [José de Anchieta](/wiki/Jos%C3%A9_de_Anchieta "José de Anchieta").[[132]](#cite_note-133) Both born on the island of Tenerife, they were respectively missionaries in [Guatemala](/wiki/Guatemala "Guatemala") and [Brazil](/wiki/Brazil "Brazil").

The Canary Islands are divided into two Catholic dioceses, each governed by a bishop:

* [Diócesis Canariense](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_Diocese_of_Canarias "Roman Catholic Diocese of Canarias"): Includes the islands of the [Eastern Province](/wiki/Province_of_Las_Palmas "Province of Las Palmas"): Gran Canaria, Fuerteventura and Lanzarote. Its capital was San Marcial El Rubicón (1404) and [Las Palmas de Gran Canaria](/wiki/Las_Palmas "Las Palmas") (1483–present). There was a previous bishopric which was based in [Telde](/wiki/Telde "Telde"), but it was later abolished.
* [Diócesis Nivariense](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_Diocese_of_San_Crist%C3%B3bal_de_La_Laguna "Roman Catholic Diocese of San Cristóbal de La Laguna"): Includes the islands of the [western province](/wiki/Santa_Cruz_de_Tenerife_(province) "Santa Cruz de Tenerife (province)"): Tenerife, La Palma, La Gomera and El Hierro. Its capital is [San Cristóbal de La Laguna](/wiki/San_Crist%C3%B3bal_de_La_Laguna "San Cristóbal de La Laguna") (1819–present).

#### Other religions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Canary_Islands&action=edit&section=37 "Edit section: Other religions")]

Separate from the overwhelming Catholic majority are a minority of [Muslims](/wiki/Muslims "Muslims").[[133]](#cite_note-laopinion.es-134) Among the followers of [Islam](/wiki/Islam "Islam"), the [Islamic Federation of the Canary Islands](/wiki/Islamic_Federation_of_the_Canary_Islands "Islamic Federation of the Canary Islands") exists to represent the Islamic community in the Canary Islands as well as to provide practical support to members of the Islamic community.[[134]](#cite_note-135) For its part, there is also the [Evangelical Council of the Canary Islands](/w/index.php?title=Evangelical_Council_of_the_Canary_Islands&action=edit&redlink=1 "Evangelical Council of the Canary Islands (page does not exist)") in the archipelago.

Other religious faiths represented include [Jehovah's Witnesses](/wiki/Jehovah%27s_Witnesses "Jehovah's Witnesses"), [The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints](/wiki/The_Church_of_Jesus_Christ_of_Latter-day_Saints "The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints") as well as [Hinduism](/wiki/Hinduism "Hinduism").[[133]](#cite_note-laopinion.es-134) Minority religions are also present such as the [Church of the Guanche People](/wiki/Church_of_the_Guanche_People "Church of the Guanche People") which is classified as a [neo-pagan](/wiki/Modern_Paganism "Modern Paganism") native religion.[[133]](#cite_note-laopinion.es-134) Also present are [Buddhism](/wiki/Buddhism "Buddhism"),[[133]](#cite_note-laopinion.es-134) [Judaism](/wiki/Judaism "Judaism"),[[133]](#cite_note-laopinion.es-134) [Baháʼí](/wiki/Bah%C3%A1%CA%BC%C3%AD_Faith "Baháʼí Faith"),[[133]](#cite_note-laopinion.es-134) [African religion](/wiki/African_religion "African religion"),[[133]](#cite_note-laopinion.es-134) and [Chinese religions](/wiki/Chinese_religions "Chinese religions").[[133]](#cite_note-laopinion.es-134)

#### Statistics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Canary_Islands&action=edit&section=38 "Edit section: Statistics")]

The distribution of beliefs in 2012 according to the CIS Barometer Autonomy was as follows:[[135]](#cite_note-136)

* Catholic 84.9%
* Atheist/Agnostic/Unbeliever 12.3%
* Other religions 1.7%

### Population genetics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Canary_Islands&action=edit&section=39 "Edit section: Population genetics")]

Main article: [Canarian people § Population genetics](/wiki/Canarian_people#Population_genetics "Canarian people")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/5d/Grancanaria197227_hg.jpg/250px-Grancanaria197227_hg.jpg)](/wiki/File:Grancanaria197227_hg.jpg)

Canarian women singing in [Gran Canaria](/wiki/Gran_Canaria "Gran Canaria"), 1972

The native inhabitants of the Canary Islands hold a gene pool that is predominantly European and native Guanche. It was found that Guanche males contributed less to the gene pool of modern Canary Islanders than Guanche females. Haplogroups typical among the Guanche have been found at high frequencies in [Latin America](/wiki/Latin_America "Latin America"), suggesting that descendants of the Guanche played an active role in the [Spanish colonization of the Americas](/wiki/Spanish_colonization_of_the_Americas "Spanish colonization of the Americas").[[136]](#cite_note-137)

In 2017, the first genome-wide data from the Guanches confirmed a North African origin and that they were genetically most similar to [ancient North African Berber peoples](/wiki/Genetic_history_of_North_Africa "Genetic history of North Africa") of the nearby North African mainland. It also showed that modern inhabitants of Gran Canaria carry an estimated 16%–31% Guanche autosomal ancestry.[[137]](#cite_note-cell_31257-5-138) A 2018 genetic study found that the Canarian population is, on average at an autosomal level, 75–83% European, 17–23% North African and 3% Sub-saharan.[[138]](#cite_note-139)

## Economy and environment

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Canary_Islands&action=edit&section=40 "Edit section: Economy and environment")]

See also: [Tourism in the Canary Islands](/wiki/Tourism_in_the_Canary_Islands "Tourism in the Canary Islands"), [List of companies based in the Canary Islands](/wiki/List_of_companies_based_in_the_Canary_Islands "List of companies based in the Canary Islands"), and [Island country § Natural resources](/wiki/Island_country#Natural_resources "Island country")

**[Tourism in the Canary Islands](/wiki/Tourism_in_the_Canary_Islands "Tourism in the Canary Islands")**[[139]](#cite_note-140)[[140]](#cite_note-141)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/61/Dunas_de_Maspalomas.jpg/250px-Dunas_de_Maspalomas.jpg)](/wiki/File:Dunas_de_Maspalomas.jpg)

The dunes of [Maspalomas](/wiki/Maspalomas "Maspalomas") in [Gran Canaria](/wiki/Gran_Canaria "Gran Canaria") is one of the tourist attractions.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f3/La_Palma_-_San_Andres_y_Sauces_-_San_Andres_-_Calle_San_Sebasti%C3%A1n_09_ies.jpg/250px-La_Palma_-_San_Andres_y_Sauces_-_San_Andres_-_Calle_San_Sebasti%C3%A1n_09_ies.jpg)](/wiki/File:La_Palma_-_San_Andres_y_Sauces_-_San_Andres_-_Calle_San_Sebasti%C3%A1n_09_ies.jpg)

A banana plantation in [San Andrés y Sauces](/wiki/San_Andr%C3%A9s_y_Sauces "San Andrés y Sauces")

The economy is based primarily [on tourism](/wiki/Tourism_in_the_Canary_Islands "Tourism in the Canary Islands"), which makes up 32% of the GDP. The Canaries receive about 12 million tourists per year. Construction makes up nearly 20% of the GDP and tropical agriculture, primarily bananas and tobacco, are grown for export to Europe and the Americas. Ecologists are concerned that the resources, especially in the more [arid](/wiki/Arid "Arid") islands, are being [overexploited](/wiki/Overexploited "Overexploited") but there are still many agricultural resources like [tomatoes](/wiki/Tomato "Tomato"), [potatoes](/wiki/Potato "Potato"), [onions](/wiki/Onion "Onion"), [cochineal](/wiki/Cochineal "Cochineal"), [sugarcane](/wiki/Sugarcane "Sugarcane"), [grapes](/wiki/Grape "Grape"), [vines](/wiki/Vine "Vine"), [dates](/wiki/Phoenix_dactylifera "Phoenix dactylifera"), [oranges](/wiki/Orange_(fruit) "Orange (fruit)"), [lemons](/wiki/Lemon "Lemon"), [figs](/wiki/Ficus "Ficus"), [wheat](/wiki/Wheat "Wheat"), [barley](/wiki/Barley "Barley"), [maize](/wiki/Maize "Maize"), [apricots](/wiki/Apricot "Apricot"), [peaches](/wiki/Peach "Peach") and [almonds](/wiki/Almond "Almond").

Water resources are also being overexploited, due to the high water usage by tourists.[[141]](#cite_note-142) Also, some islands (such as Gran Canaria and Tenerife) [overexploit the ground water](/wiki/Groundwater_extraction "Groundwater extraction"). This is done in such degree that, according to European and Spanish legal regulations, the current situation is not acceptable. To address the problems, good governance and a change in the water use paradigm have been proposed. These solutions depend largely on [controlling water use](/wiki/Water_conservation "Water conservation") and on demand management. As this is administratively difficult and politically unpalatable, most action is currently directed at increasing the public offer of water through import from outside; a decision which is economically, politically and environmentally questionable.[[142]](#cite_note-143)

To bring in revenue for environmental protection, innovation, training and water sanitation a [tourist tax](/wiki/Tourist_tax "Tourist tax") was considered in 2018, along with a doubling of the [ecotax](/wiki/Ecotax "Ecotax") and restrictions on holiday rents in the zones with the greatest pressure of demand.[[143]](#cite_note-144)

The economy is [€](/wiki/Euro "Euro") 25 billion (2001 GDP figures). The islands experienced continuous growth during a 20-year period, up until 2001, at a rate of approximately 5% annually. This growth was fueled mainly by huge amounts of [foreign direct investment](/wiki/Foreign_direct_investment "Foreign direct investment"), mostly to develop tourism real estate (hotels and apartments), and European Funds (near €11 billion in the period from 2000 to 2007), since the Canary Islands are labelled Region Objective 1 (eligible for euro structural funds).[[144]](#cite_note-145)[[145]](#cite_note-146) Additionally, the EU allows the Canary Islands Government to offer special tax concessions for investors who incorporate under the Zona Especial Canaria (ZEC) regime and create more than five jobs.[[146]](#cite_note-147)[[147]](#cite_note-148)

Spain gave permission in August 2014 for [Repsol](/wiki/Repsol "Repsol") and its partners to explore [oil](/wiki/Petroleum "Petroleum") and [natural gas](/wiki/Natural_gas "Natural gas") prospects off the Canary Islands, involving an investment of €7.5 billion over four years, to commence at the end of 2016. Repsol at the time said the area could ultimately produce 100,000 barrels of oil a day, which would meet 10 percent of Spain's energy needs.[[148]](#cite_note-RepsolCanary-149) However, the analysis of samples obtained did not show the necessary volume nor quality to consider future extraction, and the project was scrapped.[[149]](#cite_note-150)

Despite currently having very high dependence on fossil fuels, research on the renewable energy potential concluded that a high potential for renewable energy technologies exists on the archipelago. This, in such extent even that a scenario pathway to 100% renewable energy supply by 2050 has been put forward.[[150]](#cite_note-151)

The Canary Islands have great natural attractions, climate and [beaches](/wiki/Beach "Beach") make the islands a major tourist destination, being visited each year by about 12 million people (11,986,059 in 2007, noting 29% of [Britons](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom"), 22% of Spanish (from outside the Canaries), and 21% of [Germans](/wiki/Germany "Germany")). Among the islands, Tenerife has the largest number of tourists received annually, followed by Gran Canaria and Lanzarote.[[17]](#cite_note-gobiernodecanarias1-17) The archipelago's principal tourist attraction is the [Teide National Park](/wiki/Teide_National_Park "Teide National Park") (in Tenerife) where the highest mountain in Spain and third largest [volcano](/wiki/Volcano "Volcano") in the world (Mount [Teide](/wiki/Teide "Teide")), receives over 2.8 million visitors annually.[[151]](#cite_note-webtenerife2-152)

The combination of high mountains, proximity to Europe, and clean air has made the [Roque de los Muchachos](/wiki/Roque_de_los_Muchachos "Roque de los Muchachos") peak (on La Palma island) a leading location for [telescopes](/wiki/Telescope "Telescope") like the [Grantecan](/wiki/Grantecan "Grantecan").

The islands, as an autonomous region of Spain, are in the [European Union](/wiki/European_Union "European Union") and the [Schengen Area](/wiki/Schengen_Area "Schengen Area"). They are in the [European Union Customs Union](/wiki/European_Union_Customs_Union "European Union Customs Union") but outside the [VAT](/wiki/VAT "VAT") area.[[152]](#cite_note-territories-153) Instead of VAT there is a local Sales Tax (*IGIC*) which has a general rate of 7%, an increased tax rate of 13.5%, a reduced tax rate of 3% and a zero tax rate for certain basic need products and services. Consequently, some products are subject to additional VAT if being exported from the islands into mainland Spain or the rest of the EU.
This VAT plus transportation costs make very difficult that small Canarian shops sell to the rest of Spain.
The transportation costs are even higher outside of Tenerife and Gran Canaria.[[153]](#cite_note-Jiménez-154)

Canarian time is [Western European Time](/wiki/Western_European_Time "Western European Time") (WET), or [GMT](/wiki/Greenwich_Mean_Time "Greenwich Mean Time"). In summer, one hour ahead of GMT. Canarian time is one hour behind mainland Spain, and the same time as the UK, Ireland and mainland Portugal all year round.

## Transport

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Canary_Islands&action=edit&section=43 "Edit section: Transport")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a8/EC-NFA_Embraer_E195-E2_Binter_VGO_02.jpg/250px-EC-NFA_Embraer_E195-E2_Binter_VGO_02.jpg)](/wiki/File:EC-NFA_Embraer_E195-E2_Binter_VGO_02.jpg)

A [Binter Canarias](/wiki/Binter_Canarias "Binter Canarias") [Embraer 195 E2](/wiki/Embraer_195-E2 "Embraer 195-E2") at the Galician airport of [Vigo](/wiki/Vigo%E2%80%93Peinador_Airport "Vigo–Peinador Airport"). Binter is the biggest airline of the Canary Islands and labels itself as the flag carrier of the Autonomous Community (*Líneas Aéreas de Canarias*).

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8c/Las_palmas_gran_canaria_parque_san_telmo_2005.jpg/250px-Las_palmas_gran_canaria_parque_san_telmo_2005.jpg)](/wiki/File:Las_palmas_gran_canaria_parque_san_telmo_2005.jpg)

Bus Station—*Estación de Guaguas* also known as *El Hoyo* (The hole), on the left, out of the image—at San Telmo Park, [Las Palmas de Gran Canaria](/wiki/Las_Palmas_de_Gran_Canaria "Las Palmas de Gran Canaria")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/38/Tranviadetenerife.JPG/250px-Tranviadetenerife.JPG)](/wiki/File:Tranviadetenerife.JPG)

[Tenerife Tram](/wiki/Tenerife_Tram "Tenerife Tram"), is the only existing tramway or train in the Canary Islands

The Canary Islands have eight airports altogether, two of the main ports of Spain, and an extensive network of [autopistas](/wiki/Highways_in_Spain "Highways in Spain") (highways) and other roads. For a road map see multimap.[[156]](#cite_note-157) [Traffic congestion](/wiki/Traffic_congestion "Traffic congestion") is sometimes a problem in Tenerife and on Gran Canaria.[[157]](#cite_note-158)[[158]](#cite_note-159)[[159]](#cite_note-160)

Large [ferry](/wiki/Ferry "Ferry") boats and fast ferries link most of the islands. Both types can transport large numbers of passengers, cargo, and vehicles. Fast ferries are made of aluminium and powered by modern and efficient diesel engines, while conventional ferries have a steel hull and are powered by heavy oil. Fast ferries travel in excess of 30 kn (56 km/h; 35 mph); conventional ferries travel in excess of 20 kn (37 km/h; 23 mph), but are slower than fast ferries.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] A typical ferry ride between La Palma and Tenerife may take up to eight hours or more while a fast ferry takes about two and a half hours and between Tenerife and Gran Canaria can be about one hour.[[160]](#cite_note-161)

The largest airport is the [Gran Canaria Airport](/wiki/Gran_Canaria_Airport "Gran Canaria Airport"). Tenerife has two airports, [Tenerife North Airport](/wiki/Tenerife_North_Airport "Tenerife North Airport") and [Tenerife South Airport](/wiki/Tenerife_South_Airport "Tenerife South Airport").[[161]](#cite_note-162) The island of Tenerife gathers the highest passenger movement of all the Canary Islands through its two airports.[[162]](#cite_note-163) The two main islands (Tenerife and Gran Canaria) receive the greatest number of passengers.[[163]](#cite_note-164) Tenerife 6,204,499 passengers and Gran Canaria 5,011,176 passengers.[[164]](#cite_note-165)

The [port of Las Palmas](/wiki/Port_of_Las_Palmas "Port of Las Palmas") is first in freight traffic in the islands,[[165]](#cite_note-166) while the [port of Santa Cruz de Tenerife](/wiki/Port_of_Santa_Cruz_de_Tenerife "Port of Santa Cruz de Tenerife") is the first fishing port with approximately 7,500 tons of fish caught, according to the Spanish government publication Statistical Yearbook of State Ports. Similarly, it is the second port in Spain as regards ship traffic, only surpassed by the [Port of Algeciras](/wiki/Port_of_Algeciras "Port of Algeciras") Bay.[[166]](#cite_note-puertos.es-167) The port's facilities include a border inspection post (BIP) approved by the European Union, which is responsible for inspecting all types of imports from third countries or exports to countries outside the European Economic Area. The port of [Los Cristianos](/wiki/Los_Cristianos "Los Cristianos") (Tenerife) has the greatest number of passengers recorded in the Canary Islands, followed by the port of Santa Cruz de Tenerife.[[167]](#cite_note-www2.gobiernodecanarias.org-168) The Port of Las Palmas is the third port in the islands in passengers and first in number of vehicles transported.[[167]](#cite_note-www2.gobiernodecanarias.org-168)

The [SS America](/wiki/SS_America_(1939) "SS America (1939)") was beached at the Canary islands on 18 January 1994. However, the ocean liner broke apart after the course of several years and eventually sank beneath the surface.

### Fauna

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Canary_Islands&action=edit&section=49 "Edit section: Fauna")]

See also: [List of birds of the Canary Islands](/wiki/List_of_birds_of_the_Canary_Islands "List of birds of the Canary Islands"), [List of reptiles of the Canary Islands](/wiki/List_of_reptiles_of_the_Canary_Islands "List of reptiles of the Canary Islands"), [List of Lepidoptera of the Canary Islands](/wiki/List_of_Lepidoptera_of_the_Canary_Islands "List of Lepidoptera of the Canary Islands"), and [List of non-marine molluscs of the Canary Islands](/wiki/List_of_non-marine_molluscs_of_the_Canary_Islands "List of non-marine molluscs of the Canary Islands")

The bird life includes European and African species, such as the [black-bellied sandgrouse](/wiki/Black-bellied_sandgrouse "Black-bellied sandgrouse"), [canary](/wiki/Atlantic_canary "Atlantic canary"), graja – a subspecies of [red-billed chough](/wiki/Red-billed_chough "Red-billed chough") endemic to La Palma, [Gran Canaria blue chaffinch](/wiki/Gran_Canaria_blue_chaffinch "Gran Canaria blue chaffinch"), [Tenerife blue chaffinch](/wiki/Tenerife_blue_chaffinch "Tenerife blue chaffinch"), [Canary Islands chaffinch](/wiki/Canary_Islands_chaffinch "Canary Islands chaffinch"), [Canary Islands chiffchaff](/wiki/Canary_Islands_chiffchaff "Canary Islands chiffchaff"), [Fuerteventura chat](/wiki/Fuerteventura_chat "Fuerteventura chat"), [Tenerife goldcrest](/wiki/Tenerife_goldcrest "Tenerife goldcrest"), [Canarian Egyptian vulture](/wiki/Canarian_Egyptian_vulture "Canarian Egyptian vulture"), [Bolle's pigeon](/wiki/Bolle%27s_pigeon "Bolle's pigeon"), [laurel pigeon](/wiki/Laurel_pigeon "Laurel pigeon"), [plain swift](/wiki/Plain_swift "Plain swift"), and [houbara bustard](/wiki/Houbara_bustard "Houbara bustard").

Terrestrial fauna includes the [El Hierro giant lizard](/wiki/El_Hierro_giant_lizard "El Hierro giant lizard"), [Tachina canariensis](/wiki/Tachina_canariensis "Tachina canariensis"), [La Gomera giant lizard](/wiki/La_Gomera_giant_lizard "La Gomera giant lizard"), and the (possibly extinct) [La Palma giant lizard](/wiki/La_Palma_giant_lizard "La Palma giant lizard"). Mammals include the [Canarian shrew](/wiki/Canarian_shrew "Canarian shrew"), [Canary big-eared bat](/wiki/Canary_big-eared_bat "Canary big-eared bat"), the [Algerian hedgehog](/wiki/Algerian_hedgehog "Algerian hedgehog"), and the more recently introduced [mouflon](/wiki/Mouflon "Mouflon").

#### Extinct fauna

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Canary_Islands&action=edit&section=50 "Edit section: Extinct fauna")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/ce/Canariomys_bravoi_skull.JPG/250px-Canariomys_bravoi_skull.JPG)](/wiki/File:Canariomys_bravoi_skull.JPG)

Skull of [Tenerife giant rat](/wiki/Tenerife_giant_rat "Tenerife giant rat") (*Canariomys bravoi*), an endemic species that is now extinct

The Canary Islands were previously inhabited by a variety of endemic animals, such as extinct giant lizards (*[Gallotia goliath](/wiki/Gallotia_goliath "Gallotia goliath")*), giant tortoises (*[Centrochelys burchardi](/wiki/Centrochelys_burchardi "Centrochelys burchardi")* and *[C. vulcanica](/wiki/Centrochelys_vulcanica "Centrochelys vulcanica")*),[[173]](#cite_note-174) [Tenerife](/wiki/Tenerife_giant_rat "Tenerife giant rat") and [Gran Canaria](/wiki/Gran_Canaria_giant_rat "Gran Canaria giant rat") giant rats (*Canariomys bravoi* and *C. tamarani*),[[174]](#cite_note-175) and the [lava mouse](/wiki/Lava_mouse "Lava mouse") *Malpaisomys insularis*. The [Canary Islands oystercatcher](/wiki/Canary_Islands_oystercatcher "Canary Islands oystercatcher") went extinct in the 20th century, and extinct birds known only from [Pleistocene](/wiki/Pleistocene "Pleistocene") and [Holocene](/wiki/Holocene "Holocene") age bones include the [Canary Islands quail](/wiki/Canary_Islands_quail "Canary Islands quail") (*Coturnix gomerae*), [dune shearwater](/wiki/Dune_shearwater "Dune shearwater") (*Puffinus holeae*), [lava shearwater](/wiki/Lava_shearwater "Lava shearwater") (*P. olsoni*), [Trias greenfinch](/wiki/Trias_greenfinch "Trias greenfinch") (*Chloris triasi*), [slender-billed greenfinch](/wiki/Slender-billed_greenfinch "Slender-billed greenfinch") (*C. aurelioi*) and the [long-legged bunting](/wiki/Long-legged_bunting "Long-legged bunting") (*Emberiza alcoveri*).[[175]](#cite_note-176)

### Marine life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Canary_Islands&action=edit&section=51 "Edit section: Marine life")]

Main article: [Marine life of the Canary Islands](/wiki/Marine_life_of_the_Canary_Islands "Marine life of the Canary Islands")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/53/Caretta_caretta_060417w2.jpg/250px-Caretta_caretta_060417w2.jpg)](/wiki/File:Caretta_caretta_060417w2.jpg)

A [loggerhead sea turtle](/wiki/Loggerhead_sea_turtle "Loggerhead sea turtle"), by far the most common species of marine turtle in the Canary Islands

The [marine life](/wiki/Marine_life "Marine life") found in the Canary Islands is also varied, being a combination of [North Atlantic](/wiki/Atlantic_Ocean "Atlantic Ocean"), [Mediterranean](/wiki/Mediterranean_Sea "Mediterranean Sea") and [endemic](/wiki/Endemism "Endemism") species. In recent years, the increasing popularity of both [scuba diving](/wiki/Scuba_diving "Scuba diving") and [underwater photography](/wiki/Underwater_photography "Underwater photography") have provided biologists with much new information on the marine life of the islands.

Fish species found in the islands include many species of [shark](/wiki/Shark "Shark"), [ray](/wiki/Batoidea "Batoidea"), [moray eel](/wiki/Moray_eel "Moray eel"), [bream](/wiki/Sparidae "Sparidae"), [jack](/wiki/Carangidae "Carangidae"), [grunt](/wiki/Grunt-fish "Grunt-fish"), [scorpionfish](/wiki/Scorpionfish "Scorpionfish"), [triggerfish](/wiki/Triggerfish "Triggerfish"), [grouper](/wiki/Grouper "Grouper"), [goby](/wiki/Goby "Goby"), and [blenny](/wiki/Blenny "Blenny"). In addition, there are many invertebrate species, including [sponge](/wiki/Sponge "Sponge"), [jellyfish](/wiki/Jellyfish "Jellyfish"), [anemone](/wiki/Sea_anemone "Sea anemone"), [crab](/wiki/Crab "Crab"), [mollusc](/wiki/Mollusc "Mollusc"), [sea urchin](/wiki/Sea_urchin "Sea urchin"), [starfish](/wiki/Starfish "Starfish"), [sea cucumber](/wiki/Holothuroidea "Holothuroidea") and [coral](/wiki/Coral "Coral").

There are five species of [marine turtle](/wiki/Marine_turtle "Marine turtle") that are sighted periodically in the islands, the most common of these being the [endangered](/wiki/Endangered "Endangered") [loggerhead sea turtle](/wiki/Loggerhead_sea_turtle "Loggerhead sea turtle").[[176]](#cite_note-177) The other four are the [green](/wiki/Green_sea_turtle "Green sea turtle"), [hawksbill](/wiki/Hawksbill_sea_turtle "Hawksbill sea turtle"), [leatherback](/wiki/Leatherback_sea_turtle "Leatherback sea turtle") and [Kemp's ridley](/wiki/Kemp%27s_ridley_sea_turtle "Kemp's ridley sea turtle") sea turtles. Currently, there are no signs that any of these species breed in the islands, and so those seen in the water are usually [migrating](/wiki/Sea_turtle_migration "Sea turtle migration"). However, it is believed that some of these species may have bred in the islands in the past, and there are records of several sightings of leatherback sea turtle on beaches in Fuerteventura, adding credibility to the theory.

Marine mammals include the large varieties of [cetaceans](/wiki/Cetacean "Cetacean") including rare and not well-known species (see more details in the [Marine life of the Canary Islands](/wiki/Marine_life_of_the_Canary_Islands "Marine life of the Canary Islands")). [Hooded seals](/wiki/Hooded_seals "Hooded seals")[[177]](#cite_note-178) have also been known to be [vagrant](/wiki/Vagrancy_(biology) "Vagrancy (biology)") in the Canary Islands every now and then. The Canary Islands were also formerly home to a population of the rarest [pinniped](/wiki/Pinniped "Pinniped") in the world, the [Mediterranean monk seal](/wiki/Mediterranean_monk_seal "Mediterranean monk seal").

## Holidays

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Canary_Islands&action=edit&section=53 "Edit section: Holidays")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/95/Danza_de_Enanos_2015_PMBV.jpg/250px-Danza_de_Enanos_2015_PMBV.jpg)](/wiki/File:Danza_de_Enanos_2015_PMBV.jpg)

The Dance of the Dwarves is one of the most important acts of the Lustral Festivities of the *Bajada de la Virgen de las Nieves* in [Santa Cruz de La Palma](/wiki/Santa_Cruz_de_La_Palma "Santa Cruz de La Palma").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/56/Bailarines.jpg/250px-Bailarines.jpg)](/wiki/File:Bailarines.jpg)

Dancers with typical costume in El Tamaduste ([El Hierro](/wiki/El_Hierro "El Hierro"))

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/78/Banda_de_Agaete_en_la_Tra%C3%ADda_del_Agua.jpg/250px-Banda_de_Agaete_en_la_Tra%C3%ADda_del_Agua.jpg)](/wiki/File:Banda_de_Agaete_en_la_Tra%C3%ADda_del_Agua.jpg)

Band of [Agaete](/wiki/Agaete "Agaete") in the *Traída del Agua* ([Gran Canaria](/wiki/Gran_Canaria "Gran Canaria"))

Some holidays of those celebrated in the Canary Islands are international and national, others are regional holidays and others are of insular character. The official day of the autonomous community is [Canary Islands Day](/wiki/Canary_Islands_Day "Canary Islands Day") on 30 May. The anniversary of the first session of the [Parliament of the Canary Islands](/wiki/Parliament_of_the_Canary_Islands "Parliament of the Canary Islands"), based in the city of [Santa Cruz de Tenerife](/wiki/Santa_Cruz_de_Tenerife "Santa Cruz de Tenerife"), held on 30 May 1983, is commemorated with this day.

The common festive calendar throughout the Canary Islands is as follows:[[178]](#cite_note-179)

| Date | Name | Data |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 1 January | [New Year](/wiki/New_Year "New Year") | International festival. |
| 6 January | [Epiphany](/wiki/Epiphany_(holiday) "Epiphany (holiday)") | Catholic festival. |
| March or April | [Holy Thursday](/wiki/Holy_Thursday "Holy Thursday") and [Holy Friday](/wiki/Holy_Friday "Holy Friday") | Christian festival. |
| 1 May | [International Workers' Day](/wiki/International_Workers%27_Day "International Workers' Day") | International festival. |
| 30 May | [Canary Islands Day](/wiki/Canary_Islands_Day "Canary Islands Day") | Day of the autonomous community. Anniversary of the first session of the [Parliament of the Canary Islands](/wiki/Parliament_of_the_Canary_Islands "Parliament of the Canary Islands"). |
| 15 August | [Assumption of Mary](/wiki/Assumption_of_Mary "Assumption of Mary") | Catholic festival. This day is festive in the archipelago as in all of Spain. Popularly, in the Canary Islands it is known as the day on which the [Virgin of Candelaria](/wiki/Virgin_of_Candelaria "Virgin of Candelaria") (Saint Patron of Canary Islands) is celebrated.[[179]](#cite_note-180)[[180]](#cite_note-181) |
| 12 October | [Fiesta Nacional de España](/wiki/Fiesta_Nacional_de_Espa%C3%B1a "Fiesta Nacional de España") (*Día de la Hispanidad*) | National Holiday of Spain. Commemoration of [discovery of the Americas](/wiki/Discovery_of_the_Americas "Discovery of the Americas"). |
| 1 November | [All Saints' Day](/wiki/All_Saints%27_Day "All Saints' Day") | Catholic festival. |
| 6 December | [Constitution Day](/wiki/Constitution_Day "Constitution Day") | Commemoration of the [Spanish constitutional referendum, 1978](/wiki/Spanish_constitutional_referendum,_1978 "Spanish constitutional referendum, 1978"). |
| 8 December | [Immaculate Conception](/wiki/Immaculate_Conception "Immaculate Conception") | Catholic festival. The Immaculate Conception is the Saint Patron of Spain. |
| 25 December | [Christmas](/wiki/Christmas "Christmas") | Christian festival. Commemoration of the birth of [Jesus of Nazareth](/wiki/Jesus_of_Nazareth "Jesus of Nazareth"). |

In addition, each of the islands has an island festival, in which it is a holiday only on that specific island. These are the festivities of island patrons saints of each island. Organized chronologically are:[[181]](#cite_note-182)

| Date | Island | Saint/Virgin |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 2 February | [Tenerife](/wiki/Tenerife "Tenerife") | [Our Lady of Candelaria](/wiki/Our_Lady_of_Candelaria "Our Lady of Candelaria") |
| 5 August | [La Palma](/wiki/La_Palma "La Palma") | [Our Lady of the Snows](/wiki/Dedication_of_the_Basilica_of_St_Mary_Major "Dedication of the Basilica of St Mary Major") |
| 8 September | [Gran Canaria](/wiki/Gran_Canaria "Gran Canaria") | [Our Lady of the Pine](/wiki/Virgen_del_Pino "Virgen del Pino") |
| 15 September | [Lanzarote](/wiki/Lanzarote "Lanzarote") | [Our Lady of Dolours](/wiki/Our_Lady_of_Sorrows "Our Lady of Sorrows") |
| Third Saturday of the month of September | [Fuerteventura](/wiki/Fuerteventura "Fuerteventura") | [Our Lady of the Peña](/w/index.php?title=Our_Lady_of_the_Pe%C3%B1a&action=edit&redlink=1 "Our Lady of the Peña (page does not exist)") |
| 24 September | [El Hierro](/wiki/El_Hierro "El Hierro") | [Our Lady of the Kings](/w/index.php?title=Our_Lady_of_the_Kings&action=edit&redlink=1 "Our Lady of the Kings (page does not exist)") |
| Monday following the first Saturday of October | [La Gomera](/wiki/La_Gomera "La Gomera") | [Our Lady of Guadalupe](/wiki/Our_Lady_of_Guadalupe "Our Lady of Guadalupe") |

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/11/Desfile_del_S%C3%A1bado_de_Carnaval%2C_en_Santa_Cruz_de_Tenerife.JPG/250px-Desfile_del_S%C3%A1bado_de_Carnaval%2C_en_Santa_Cruz_de_Tenerife.JPG)](/wiki/File:Desfile_del_S%C3%A1bado_de_Carnaval,_en_Santa_Cruz_de_Tenerife.JPG)

Parade in the [Carnival of Santa Cruz de Tenerife](/wiki/Carnival_of_Santa_Cruz_de_Tenerife "Carnival of Santa Cruz de Tenerife")

The most famous festivals of the Canary Islands is the carnival. It is the most famous and international festival of the archipelago. The carnival is celebrated in all the islands and all its municipalities, perhaps the two busiest are those of the two Canarian capitals; the [Carnival of Santa Cruz de Tenerife](/wiki/Carnival_of_Santa_Cruz_de_Tenerife "Carnival of Santa Cruz de Tenerife") (*Tourist Festival of International Interest*) and the [Carnival of Las Palmas de Gran Canaria](/wiki/Carnival_of_Las_Palmas_de_Gran_Canaria "Carnival of Las Palmas de Gran Canaria"). It is celebrated on the streets between the months of February and March. But the rest of the islands of the archipelago have their carnivals with their own traditions among which stand out: The Festival of the Carneros of [El Hierro](/wiki/El_Hierro "El Hierro"), the Festival of the Diabletes of [Teguise](/wiki/Teguise_(municipality) "Teguise (municipality)") in [Lanzarote](/wiki/Lanzarote "Lanzarote"), Los Indianos de [La Palma](/wiki/La_Palma "La Palma"), the Carnival of [San Sebastián de La Gomera](/wiki/San_Sebasti%C3%A1n_de_La_Gomera "San Sebastián de La Gomera") and the Carnival of [Puerto del Rosario](/wiki/Puerto_del_Rosario "Puerto del Rosario") in [Fuerteventura](/wiki/Fuerteventura "Fuerteventura").

## Science and technology

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Canary_Islands&action=edit&section=54 "Edit section: Science and technology")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/27/Ing_telescopes_sunset_la_palma_july_2001.jpg/250px-Ing_telescopes_sunset_la_palma_july_2001.jpg)](/wiki/File:Ing_telescopes_sunset_la_palma_july_2001.jpg)

[Roque de los Muchachos Observatory](/wiki/Roque_de_los_Muchachos_Observatory "Roque de los Muchachos Observatory"), La Palma

In the 1960s, Gran Canaria was selected as the location for one of the 14 [ground stations](/wiki/Ground_stations "Ground stations") in the [Manned Space Flight Network](/wiki/Manned_Space_Flight_Network "Manned Space Flight Network") (MSFN) to support the [NASA](/wiki/NASA "NASA") space program. [Maspalomas Station](/wiki/Maspalomas_Station "Maspalomas Station"), located in the south of the island, took part in a number of space missions including the [Apollo 11](/wiki/Apollo_11 "Apollo 11") Moon landings and [Skylab](/wiki/Skylab "Skylab"). Today it continues to support satellite communications as part of the [ESA](/wiki/ESA "ESA") network.[[182]](#cite_note-esa-page-183)

Because of the remote location, a number of [astronomical observatories](/wiki/Observatory "Observatory") are located in the archipelago, including the [Teide Observatory](/wiki/Teide_Observatory "Teide Observatory") on Tenerife, the [Roque de los Muchachos Observatory](/wiki/Roque_de_los_Muchachos_Observatory "Roque de los Muchachos Observatory") on La Palma, and the [Temisas Astronomical Observatory](/wiki/Temisas_Astronomical_Observatory "Temisas Astronomical Observatory") on Gran Canaria.

Tenerife is the home of the [Instituto de Astrofísica de Canarias](/wiki/Instituto_de_Astrof%C3%ADsica_de_Canarias "Instituto de Astrofísica de Canarias") (Astrophysical Institute of the Canaries). There is also an Instituto de Bio-Orgánica Antonio González (Antonio González Bio-Organic Institute) at the [University of La Laguna](/wiki/University_of_La_Laguna "University of La Laguna"). Also at that university are the Instituto de Lingüística Andrés Bello (Andrés Bello Institute of Linguistics), the Centro de Estudios Medievales y Renacentistas (Center for Medieval and Renaissance Studies), the Instituto Universitario de la Empresa (University Institute of Business), the Instituto de Derecho Regional (Regional Institute of Law), the Instituto Universitario de Ciencias Políticas y Sociales (University Institute of Political and Social Sciences) and the Instituto de Enfermedades Tropicales (Institute of Tropical Diseases). The latter is one of the seven institutions of the Red de Investigación de Centros de Enfermedades Tropicales (RICET, "Network of Research of Centers of Tropical Diseases"), located in various parts of Spain. The Instituto Volcanológico de Canarias (Volcanological Institute of the Canary Islands) is based in Tenerife.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

## Sports

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Canary_Islands&action=edit&section=55 "Edit section: Sports")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/49/RodriguezLopez2008.jpg/250px-RodriguezLopez2008.jpg)](/wiki/File:RodriguezLopez2008.jpg)

[Heliodoro Rodríguez López Stadium](/wiki/Estadio_Heliodoro_Rodr%C3%ADguez_L%C3%B3pez "Estadio Heliodoro Rodríguez López") in Tenerife, the stadium with the largest field area in the Canary Islands[[183]](#cite_note-184)[[184]](#cite_note-185)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1d/Estadiogc7septiembre2008.jpg/250px-Estadiogc7septiembre2008.jpg)](/wiki/File:Estadiogc7septiembre2008.jpg)

[Gran Canaria Stadium](/wiki/Estadio_Gran_Canaria "Estadio Gran Canaria"), the biggest sports venue in the Canary Islands[[185]](#cite_note-186)

A unique form of wrestling known as [Canarian wrestling](/wiki/Canarian_wrestling "Canarian wrestling") (*lucha canaria*) has opponents stand in a special area called a "terrero" and try to throw each other to the ground using strength and quick movements.[[186]](#cite_note-ctspanish.com-187)

Another sport is the "game of the sticks" (palo canario) where opponents fence with long sticks. This may have come about from the shepherds of the islands who would challenge each other using their long walking sticks.[[186]](#cite_note-ctspanish.com-187)

Furthermore, there is the [shepherd's jump](/wiki/Salto_del_pastor "Salto del pastor") (*salto del pastor*). This involves using a long stick to vault over an open area. This sport possibly evolved from the shepherd's need to occasionally get over an open area in the hills as they were tending their sheep.[[186]](#cite_note-ctspanish.com-187)

The two main [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") teams in the archipelago are: the [CD Tenerife](/wiki/CD_Tenerife "CD Tenerife") (founded in 1912) and [UD Las Palmas](/wiki/UD_Las_Palmas "UD Las Palmas") (founded in 1949). As of the 2025/2026 season, UD Las Palmas plays in the Segunda División, the second tier of Spanish football. CD Tenerife however plays in The Primera Federación. When in the same division, the clubs contest the [Canary Islands derby](/wiki/Canary_Islands_derby "Canary Islands derby"). There are smaller clubs also playing in the mainland [Spanish football league system](/wiki/Spanish_football_league_system "Spanish football league system"), most notably [UD Lanzarote](/wiki/UD_Lanzarote "UD Lanzarote") and [CD Laguna](/wiki/CD_Laguna_de_Tenerife "CD Laguna de Tenerife"), although no other Canarian clubs have played in the top flight.

The mountainous terrain of the Canary Islands also caters to the growing popularity of [ultra running](/wiki/Ultra_running "Ultra running") and [ultramarathons](/wiki/Ultramarathons "Ultramarathons") as host of annual competitive long-distance events including [CajaMar Tenerife Bluetrail](/wiki/CajaMar_Tenerife_Bluetrail "CajaMar Tenerife Bluetrail") on [Tenerife](/wiki/Tenerife "Tenerife"), [Transvulcania](/wiki/Transvulcania "Transvulcania") on [La Palma](/wiki/La_Palma "La Palma"), [Transgrancanaria](/wiki/Transgrancanaria "Transgrancanaria")[[187]](#cite_note-188) on [Gran Canaria](/wiki/Gran_Canaria "Gran Canaria"), and the Half [Marathon des Sables](/wiki/Marathon_des_Sables "Marathon des Sables") on [Fuerteventura](/wiki/Fuerteventura "Fuerteventura"). A yearly [Ironman Triathlon](/wiki/Ironman_Triathlon "Ironman Triathlon") has been taking place on [Lanzarote](/wiki/Lanzarote "Lanzarote") since 1992.[[188]](#cite_note-ironman-189)[[189]](#cite_note-ironmanhistory-190)

### Notable athletes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Canary_Islands&action=edit&section=56 "Edit section: Notable athletes")]

* [Paco Campos](/wiki/Paco_Campos "Paco Campos"), (1916–1995); a footballer who played as a forward. With 127 goals, 120 of which were for [Atlético Madrid](/wiki/Atl%C3%A9tico_Madrid "Atlético Madrid"), he is the highest scoring player from the Canary Islands in [La Liga](/wiki/La_Liga "La Liga").
* [Nicolás García](/wiki/Nicol%C3%A1s_Garc%C3%ADa_(taekwondo) "Nicolás García (taekwondo)") Hemme, born 20 June 1988 in [Las Palmas de Gran Canaria](/wiki/Las_Palmas_de_Gran_Canaria "Las Palmas de Gran Canaria"), Canary Islands, [2012 London Olympics](/wiki/2012_London_Olympics "2012 London Olympics"), [Taekwondo](/wiki/Taekwondo "Taekwondo") Silver Medalist in Men's Welterweight category (−80 kg).
* [Alfredo Cabrera](/wiki/Al_Cabrera "Al Cabrera"), (1881–1964); [shortstop](/wiki/Shortstop "Shortstop") for the [St. Louis Cardinals](/wiki/St._Louis_Cardinals "St. Louis Cardinals") in 1913
* [Sergio Rodríguez](/wiki/Sergio_Rodr%C3%ADguez "Sergio Rodríguez"), born in [San Cristóbal de La Laguna](/wiki/San_Crist%C3%B3bal_de_La_Laguna "San Cristóbal de La Laguna") in 1986, played [point guard](/wiki/Point_guard "Point guard") for the [Portland Trail Blazers](/wiki/Portland_Trail_Blazers "Portland Trail Blazers"), [Sacramento Kings](/wiki/Sacramento_Kings "Sacramento Kings"), and [New York Knicks](/wiki/New_York_Knicks "New York Knicks").
* [David Silva](/wiki/David_Silva "David Silva"), born in [Arguineguín](/wiki/Arguinegu%C3%ADn "Arguineguín") in 1986, plays [association football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") for [Real Sociedad](/wiki/Real_Sociedad "Real Sociedad"), member of the [2010 FIFA World Cup](/wiki/2010_FIFA_World_Cup "2010 FIFA World Cup") champion [Spain national football team](/wiki/Spain_men%27s_national_football_team "Spain men's national football team")
* [Juan Carlos Valerón](/wiki/Juan_Carlos_Valer%C3%B3n "Juan Carlos Valerón"), born in [Arguineguín](/wiki/Arguinegu%C3%ADn "Arguineguín") in 1975, played [association football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") for [Deportivo la Coruna](/wiki/Deportivo_de_La_Coru%C3%B1a "Deportivo de La Coruña") and [Las Palmas](/wiki/UD_Las_Palmas "UD Las Palmas").
* [Pedro](/wiki/Pedro_(footballer,_born_1987) "Pedro (footballer, born 1987)"), born in [Santa Cruz de Tenerife](/wiki/Santa_Cruz_de_Tenerife "Santa Cruz de Tenerife") in 1987, plays [association football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") for [Lazio](/wiki/S.S._Lazio "S.S. Lazio"), member of the [2010 FIFA World Cup](/wiki/2010_FIFA_World_Cup "2010 FIFA World Cup") champion [Spain national football team](/wiki/Spain_men%27s_national_football_team "Spain men's national football team")
* [Carla Suárez Navarro](/wiki/Carla_Su%C3%A1rez_Navarro "Carla Suárez Navarro"), born in [Las Palmas de Gran Canaria](/wiki/Las_Palmas_de_Gran_Canaria "Las Palmas de Gran Canaria") in 1988, professional tennis player
* [Paola Tirados](/wiki/Paola_Tirados "Paola Tirados"), born in [Las Palmas de Gran Canaria](/wiki/Las_Palmas_de_Gran_Canaria "Las Palmas de Gran Canaria") in 1980, synchronized swimmer, who participated in the Olympic Games of 2000, 2004 and 2008. She won the silver medal in Beijing in 2008 in the team competition category.
* [Jesé](/wiki/Jes%C3%A9 "Jesé"), born in [Las Palmas de Gran Canaria](/wiki/Las_Palmas_de_Gran_Canaria "Las Palmas de Gran Canaria") in 1993, plays [association football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") for [Las Palmas](/wiki/UD_Las_Palmas "UD Las Palmas").
* [Christo Bezuidenhout](/wiki/Christo_Bezuidenhout "Christo Bezuidenhout"), born in [Tenerife](/wiki/Tenerife "Tenerife") in 1970, played [rugby union](/wiki/Rugby_union "Rugby union") for [Gloucester](/wiki/Gloucester_Rugby "Gloucester Rugby") and [South Africa](/wiki/South_Africa_national_rugby_union_team "South Africa national rugby union team").
* [Pedri](/wiki/Pedri "Pedri"), born in [Tegueste](/wiki/Tegueste "Tegueste") in 2002, plays [association football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") for [Barcelona](/wiki/FC_Barcelona "FC Barcelona").
* [Misa Rodríguez](/wiki/Misa_Rodr%C3%ADguez "Misa Rodríguez"), born in [Las Palmas de Gran Canaria](/wiki/Las_Palmas_de_Gran_Canaria "Las Palmas de Gran Canaria") in 1999, plays [association football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") for [Real Madrid Femenino](/wiki/Real_Madrid_Femenino "Real Madrid Femenino"). Member of the 2023 Women's World Cup winning [Spain women's national football team](/wiki/Spain_women%27s_national_football_team "Spain women's national football team").
* [Nico Paz](/wiki/Nico_Paz "Nico Paz"), born in [Santa Cruz de Tenerife](/wiki/Santa_Cruz_de_Tenerife "Santa Cruz de Tenerife") in 2004, plays [association football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") for [Como](/wiki/Como_1907 "Como 1907").
